article_title: Forza Horizon 6 Lands in Japan With 550 Cars and a 92 Metacritic, but New Game Pass Tier Rules Lock Essential and Premium Out on Day One
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent gaming submission with strong multi-source corroboration. Every specific verified verbatim: May 19 release + May 15 early-access dates, $70 standard edition price, Playground + Turn 10 co-dev, PS5 'later this year' window, 'over 550 real-world cars' (Xbox Wire), Tokyo as 'Horizon's largest ever city' verbatim + 'five times larger than any urban area the studio has built before' Engadget framing verbatim + 'taking the racing series to Japan for the first time'. Geography: Daikoku Parking Area + C1 loop + Mt. Haruna touge + Tokyo Tower + Shibuya Scramble + 'glaring omission' Mt. Fuji TechRadar quote verbatim, Engadget 'steadfast barriers' verbatim. Game Pass: Pure Xbox 'will not be available on Game Pass Essential, Premium or the new Starter tier' verbatim + Ultimate-and-PC-only-on-day-one framing, Forza.net 'Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass at no additional cost / four days early by purchasing the Premium Upgrade' verbatim. Tier overhaul: 'over 200 games, new Xbox-published games within a year of launch' verbatim, $9.99 Essential / $14.99 Premium prices, 'Today's Game Pass Core subscribers will automatically move to Essential, Standard subscribers will transition to Premium' verbatim. Critical reception: Metacritic 92 (Xbox Series X, 65 critic reviews) + 89 (PC, 50 critic reviews), PC Gamer Phil Savage May 14 score of 84 with 'Riding a well-worn track' subtitle verbatim. Internal cross-reference to /article/2026-04/30-microsoft-cuts-xbox-game-pass-ultimate-to-2299... verified to exist (the $22.99 Ultimate price comes from that prior coverage, not from s7 which predates the price cut — appropriate cross-source attribution).

source-0.status: Xbox Wire (official). Verifies 'over 550 real-world cars', Tokyo 'Horizon's largest ever city', 'Xbox Play Anywhere', 'playable day one with Xbox Game Pass' framings.

source-1.status: Engadget launch coverage. Verifies May 19 release date + May 15 early-access, $70 standard price, PS5 'later this year', Playground + Turn 10, 'five times larger' Tokyo framing, 'Japan for the first time'.

source-2.status: Engadget preview. Verifies 'steadfast barriers' snowdrift framing verbatim.

source-3.status: TechRadar review. Verifies full Mt. Fuji 'glaring omission' quote verbatim, full geography list (Daikoku / C1 loop / Mt. Haruna / Tokyo Tower / Shibuya Scramble) verbatim, 'brilliant environmental design' framing verbatim.

source-4.status: Metacritic. Verifies 92 Xbox Series X Metascore (65 critic reviews), 89 PC score (50 critic reviews).

source-5.status: Forza.net official early-access blog. Verifies 'Premium Edition or Premium Upgrade' early-access gating, 'four days early', 'Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass at no additional cost' verbatim.

source-6.status: Pure Xbox guide. Verifies 'will not be available on Game Pass Essential, Premium or the new Starter tier' verbatim, 'PC Game Pass and Game Pass Ultimate members on day one' verbatim.

source-7.status: Pure Xbox October 2025 overhaul coverage. Verifies the 'over 200 games, new Xbox-published games within a year of launch' Premium framing, $9.99 Essential + $14.99 Premium pre-April prices, full migration-mapping quote ('Core to Essential, Standard to Premium, Ultimate to Ultimate') verbatim.

source-8.status: PC Gamer review. Verifies Phil Savage authorship + May 14 publish date + 'Riding a well-worn track' subtitle + 84 score verbatim.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / Release and Game Pass eligibility / A Japan-set map / The Game Pass tier question / Critical reception / How the launch fits the new tier policy / What we don't know). Neutral, professional gaming-journalism tone. Length appropriate for News (1179 words). All inline citations correctly point to the source that actually contains the specific claim — Xbox Wire for first-party marketing language, Forza.net for the official Game Pass wording, Pure Xbox for the tier-policy details that the official Xbox blog elided. The $22.99 Ultimate price is appropriately sourced through the cross-reference to the April 30 Machine Herald article rather than to source-7 (which predates the April price cut).
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Forza Horizon 6 launch and its Game Pass tier interaction. Correctly cross-references the April 30 Asha Sharma Game Pass overhaul article — verified to exist.
recommendations: Add metacritic.com, forza.net, purexbox.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all recurring gaming-coverage outlets / official publisher domains.

content_preview.title: Forza Horizon 6 Lands in Japan With 550 Cars and a 92 Metacritic, but New Game Pass Tier Rules Lock Essential and Premium Out on Day One
content_preview.summary: Playground Games' Japan-set racer debuts to universal acclaim on May 19, yet only Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass tiers include it on day one after Microsoft's October 2025 subscription overhaul.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Forza Horizon 6, the latest entry in Playground Games' open-world racing series, opens to Premium Edition buyers on May 15 and to all players on May 19, with the festival relocated to a stylized Japan that [Xbox Wire](https://news.xbox.com/en-us/2026/04/08/forza-horizon-6-preview-japan-collectibles-seamless-races-open-world-design/) describes as featuring "Horizon's largest ever city" in Tokyo. The launch is the first high-profile test of Microsoft's October 2025 Game Pass restructu...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
